VAL-SRV-001.evidence: curl -sf http://localhost:8200/ returns HTML content (DOCTYPE html, Perplexity ASCII art in head). Server running on port 8200.

VAL-SRV-002.evidence: curl returns content for validate.html (HTML), validate-app.js (JS), data/weeks.json (JSON). All static file types served correctly.

VAL-SRV-003.evidence: POST to /api/labels/test-srv-003 with JSON body returned HTTP 200. File site/data/labels/test-srv-003.json created on disk with matching JSON content: [{"detection_id":"test-srv-003","label":"CORRECT","primitive":"displacement"}]

VAL-SRV-004.evidence: POST to /api/lock-records/test-srv-004 with JSON body returned HTTP 200. File site/data/lock-records/test-srv-004.json created on disk with matching content: {"week":"test-srv-004","notes":"test lock","params":{"displacement":{"atr_multiple":1.5}}}

VAL-SRV-005.evidence: After POST to /api/labels/test-srv-003, GET /data/labels/test-srv-003.json returned identical JSON content. Static serving correctly returns saved files.

VAL-SRV-006.evidence: First POST wrote [{"label":"FIRST"}], second POST to same endpoint wrote [{"label":"SECOND"}]. File on disk contains only SECOND content — overwrite behavior confirmed.

VAL-SRV-007.evidence: POST with plain text body 'this is not json' returned HTTP 400. Invalid JSON properly rejected.

VAL-SRV-008.evidence: POST to /api/labels/test-srv-008 returned HTTP 200 and file was created at site/data/labels/test-srv-008.json. Directory auto-creation works.
